Version history spike (§0.5 GATE) — Swiss Federal Constitution
================================================================

Target law: Bundesverfassung der Schweizerischen Eidgenossenschaft (BV)
ConsolidationAbstract URI: https://fedlex.data.admin.ch/eli/cc/1999/404
Total jolux:Consolidation nodes linked to this Abstract: 37
  (covers Jan 2000 through March 2024, one per point-in-time change)

DE manifestation format coverage (reported via SPARQL):
  pdf-a  = 37 versions (all)
  doc    = 31 versions
  xml    = 6 versions     <-- Akoma Ntoso only since 2021-01-01
  html   =  6 versions
  docx   =  6 versions

Versions extracted via SPARQL + filestore GET (DE, XML manifestation):

version 1: date 2022-02-13, 2468 <p> elements, 231 <article> elements
  URL: https://fedlex.data.admin.ch/filestore/fedlex.data.admin.ch/eli/cc/1999/404/20220213/de/xml/fedlex-data-admin-ch-eli-cc-1999-404-20220213-de-xml-14.xml
  FRBRdate jolux:dateApplicability = 2022-02-13
  FRBRdate jolux:dateEntryInForce  = 2000-01-01
  FRBRdate jolux:dateDocument       = 1999-04-18

version 2: date 2024-03-03, 2510 <p> elements, 232 <article> elements
  URL: https://fedlex.data.admin.ch/filestore/fedlex.data.admin.ch/eli/cc/1999/404/20240303/de/xml/fedlex-data-admin-ch-eli-cc-1999-404-20240303-de-xml-10.xml
  FRBRdate jolux:dateApplicability = 2024-03-03
  FRBRdate jolux:dateEntryInForce  = 2000-01-01
  FRBRdate jolux:dateDocument       = 1999-04-18

Delta between the two: +42 paragraphs, +1 article (new body content).

GATE RESULT: PASS — we can extract ≥2 distinct point-in-time versions of
a single law, each with a stable effective date (jolux:dateApplicability
from the Consolidation node, mirrored on FRBRdate in the XML itself) and
full parsed text.

IMPORTANT LIMITATION DISCOVERED
-------------------------------
Akoma Ntoso XML is NOT available for all 37 historical consolidations of
the Constitution, only for the 6 most recent (since 2021-01-01). The 31
older consolidations only exist as pdf-a + doc. The same ratio holds for
the Swiss Civil Code (ZGB, cc/24/233_245_233): 44 consolidations total
but only 11 with XML (earliest 2011-01-01).

This means: **version-history coverage for legalize-ch will be truncated
at the point where XML manifestations become available for each law**
(typically around 2011 for older laws and around 2021 for laws that
received late Akoma Ntoso conversion, with newer laws naturally full).

Options, ranked:
  A. XML-only (recommended for v1). Skip pre-XML consolidations entirely
     and document the history window per law in extra.history_since.
     Losing pre-2021 history for long-running laws is acceptable for v1
     because (i) DOC parsing is fragile, (ii) PDF parsing violates the
     "no PDF scraping except last resort" rule, (iii) newly-enacted
     laws (post-2022-05-30) are fully covered.
  B. XML for consolidations that have it, DOCX fallback for the rest.
     Far more engineering (parse two formats), but would unlock more
     history for old laws. Can be added later in a v2 pass.
  C. XML for current + all prior reforms reconstructed from FGA (Federal
     Gazette) amending acts. FGA is Akoma Ntoso from 2022-05-30 only, so
     this only helps for reforms enacted after that date.

Recommendation: ship v1 with Option A, explicitly record in
extra.history_from the earliest jolux:dateApplicability for which an XML
manifestation exists. Add a GitHub issue for Option B as a follow-up.
